audit_status.review_note: Reviewed for Abram's return from Egypt, wealth/flocks conflict, Lot's lifted-eyes choice, separation language, renewed land/seed promise, and altar at Hebron. Final source-critical review may refine geography.

verses.restored: Abram went up from Egypt, he and his woman and all that belonged to him, and Lot with him, into the Negev.

familiar.text: And Abram went up out of Egypt, he, and his wife, and all that he had, and Lot with him...

notes.title: Went up from Egypt
notes.body: The movement out of Egypt anticipates later Exodus patterns.

verses.restored: Abram was very heavy with livestock, silver, and gold.

familiar.text: And Abram was very rich in cattle, in silver, and in gold.

notes.title: Heavy/rich
notes.body: Kaved can mean heavy or weighty; here it conveys great wealth.

verses.restored: He went on his journeys from the Negev as far as Bethel, to the place where his tent had been at the beginning, between Bethel and Ai,

familiar.text: And he went on his journeys from the south even to Bethel...

verses.restored: to the place of the altar that he had made there at first. There Abram called on the name of the Eternal.

familiar.text: Unto the place of the altar, which he had made there at the first: and there Abram called on the name of the LORD.

notes.title: Return to altar
notes.body: After Egypt, Abram returns to the earlier altar and invocation of the Eternal’s name.

verses.restored: Lot also, who went with Abram, had flocks, herds, and tents.

familiar.text: And Lot also, which went with Abram, had flocks, and herds, and tents.

verses.restored: The land could not bear them dwelling together, for their possessions were many, and they were not able to dwell together.

familiar.text: And the land was not able to bear them, that they might dwell together...

notes.title: Land cannot bear them
notes.body: The abundance of possessions creates pressure on land and relationship.

verses.restored: There was strife between the herders of Abram’s livestock and the herders of Lot’s livestock. The Canaanite and the Perizzite were then dwelling in the land.

familiar.text: And there was a strife between the herdmen of Abram's cattle and the herdmen of Lot's cattle...

notes.title: Strife in occupied land
notes.body: The conflict occurs while other peoples already dwell in the land, heightening pressure.

verses.restored: Abram said to Lot, Please let there be no strife between me and you, and between my herders and your herders, for we are brothers.

familiar.text: And Abram said unto Lot, Let there be no strife, I pray thee, between me and thee... for we be brethren.

notes.title: We are brothers
notes.body: Abram appeals to kinship to de-escalate conflict.

verses.restored: Is not the whole land before you? Please separate from me. If you go left, I will go right; and if you go right, I will go left.

familiar.text: Is not the whole land before thee? separate thyself, I pray thee, from me...

notes.title: Generous separation
notes.body: Abram gives Lot first choice, yielding control rather than forcing advantage.

verses.restored: Lot lifted his eyes and saw the whole plain of the Jordan, that all of it was well-watered, before the Eternal ruined Sodom and Gomorrah, like the garden of the Eternal, like the land of Egypt as you go toward Zoar.

familiar.text: And Lot lifted up his eyes, and beheld all the plain of Jordan, that it was well watered every where...

notes.title: Lifted his eyes
notes.body: Lot chooses by sight, drawn to abundance.

notes.title: Before ruin
notes.body: The narrator foreshadows Sodom and Gomorrah’s destruction.

verses.restored: So Lot chose for himself the whole plain of the Jordan, and Lot journeyed east. They separated from one another.

familiar.text: Then Lot chose him all the plain of Jordan; and Lot journeyed east...

notes.title: Eastward movement
notes.body: Eastward movement in Genesis often carries exile or distance-from-center overtones.

verses.restored: Abram dwelled in the land of Canaan, and Lot dwelled among the cities of the plain and pitched his tent as far as Sodom.

familiar.text: Abram dwelled in the land of Canaan, and Lot dwelled in the cities of the plain...

notes.title: Toward Sodom
notes.body: Lot’s tent moves toward Sodom before the text names Sodom’s condition.

verses.restored: Now the people of Sodom were harmful and great offenders against the Eternal.

familiar.text: But the men of Sodom were wicked and sinners before the LORD exceedingly.

notes.title: Harmful and offenders
notes.body: Ra'im and chatta'im describe harmful/wicked people and offenders/sinners. The verse gives a moral warning before later Sodom narratives.

verses.restored: The Eternal said to Abram, after Lot had separated from him, Lift your eyes now and look from the place where you are: northward, southward, eastward, and westward.

familiar.text: And the LORD said unto Abram, after that Lot was separated from him, Lift up now thine eyes...

notes.title: Abram’s lifted eyes
notes.body: Lot lifted his eyes to choose; now the Eternal tells Abram to lift his eyes to receive promise.

verses.restored: For all the land that you see, I will give to you and to your seed forever.

familiar.text: For all the land which thou seest, to thee will I give it, and to thy seed for ever.

notes.title: Seed forever
notes.body: The land promise is extended to Abram’s seed with an enduring horizon.

verses.restored: I will make your seed like the dust of the earth, so that if anyone could count the dust of the earth, your seed also could be counted.

familiar.text: And I will make thy seed as the dust of the earth...

notes.title: Dust of the earth
notes.body: The seed promise uses earth/dust imagery, connecting offspring to creation and ground.

verses.restored: Rise, walk through the land, through its length and its width, for I will give it to you.

familiar.text: Arise, walk through the land in the length of it and in the breadth of it...

notes.title: Walk the land
notes.body: Abram is invited to embody the promise by walking the land.

verses.restored: Abram moved his tent and came and dwelled by the oaks of Mamre, which are in Hebron, and there he built an altar to the Eternal.

familiar.text: Then Abram removed his tent, and came and dwelt in the plain of Mamre...

notes.title: Oaks of Mamre
notes.body: Abram’s movement ends in altar-building at Mamre/Hebron, another sacred place marker.
